title: AI Workforce Build State
description: Tracks the post-interview build of every department + role so the build can be autonomously resumed if an agent session is interrupted. Lives at /data/.openclaw/workspace/.workforce-build-state.json (VPS) or ~/.openclaw/workspace/.workforce-build-state.json (Mac). Written by the agent at interview-completion AND after every unit of build work completes. Extended in v10.14.17 to also track the post-build CLOSEOUT pipeline (Skill 37 ZHC Closeout).

ownerChat.description: Owner Telegram chat ID for final status message

companyName.description: Client's company name — used by Skill 37 in prompts + Notion page titles

companySlug.description: Canonical URL/folder slug for the company (slugify of companyName, e.g. 'blackceo-llc'). Written by build-workforce.py _write_interview_complete_to_state() at interview-completion. This is the ONE key every downstream reader must consume: Skill 32 run-full-install.sh, update-skills.sh, and the Command Center repo scripts all key on companySlug (with a transition fallback to clientSlug). Previously build-workforce.py only wrote it into company-config.json, so state-file readers resolved empty. Added in the PRD review sprint (P1-3).

clientSlug.description: TRANSITION ALIAS of companySlug (P1-3). build-workforce.py writes BOTH companySlug and clientSlug with the same value for one release so state files written by an older reader-set still resolve. Readers use the fallback chain `.companySlug // .clientSlug`. Prefer companySlug; clientSlug will be dropped once every reader is on the fallback chain.

industry.description: Client's industry — used by Skill 37 to pick example tasks

buildPid.description: Optional lock token to prevent concurrent builds

buildCompletedAt.description: PRD-3.3 R3.3 (v12.4.x auto-closeout): the build-completion timestamp. Written AUTOMATICALLY BY A SCRIPT (resume-workforce-build.sh) the moment every department is done + roleLibraryStatus==done + sopLibraryStatus==done + commsAutomationStatus terminal, so the closeout fires without an agent hand-write. The agent's inline write is a redundant faster path (idempotent: the cron only writes when this is empty). Previously this was an agent-only hand-write with no script writer (the missing HOP-4, diag/03).

buildKickRequestedAt.description: PRD-3.3 R3.1 (v12.4.x auto-closeout): ISO timestamp written by update-interview-state.sh --complete when it seeds the build/closeout gate fields and fires the internal [WORKFORCE-RESUME] build-kick self-ping. Makes 'interview finished' a state-driven trigger that starts the build immediately instead of waiting on a second agent hand-write. Auditable + idempotent (re-running --complete refreshes it; the build-kick is skipped when departments are already underway).

filesPlanned.description: Expected file list (e.g. README.md, AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, TOOLS.md, USER.md, MEMORY.md, HEARTBEAT.md)

agentRegistered.description: True only when a corresponding entry exists in openclaw.json agents.list[] with id == 'dept-<slug>'. Materialized by Skill 32's materialize-dept-agents.sh. A dept with status=done but agentRegistered=false is a LIE — the role-definition.md files exist but the OpenClaw runtime sees no such agent. Added v10.14.19.

roleLibraryFilled.description: True only when EVERY role folder in this dept has its how-to.md filled from the role library (carries the '<!-- Filled from role-library v' marker), as verified by qc-completeness.sh (library_pct == 100). A dept with status=done but roleLibraryFilled=false is INCOMPLETE — the role scaffolds exist but the role library was never pulled in. Added v10.15.8. ENFORCED: see roleLibraryStatus + the verify/resume gate.

sopLibraryFilled.description: True only when EVERY role folder in this dept has its SOP files authored/pulled (no remaining 'to be personalized' stubs), as verified by qc-completeness.sh (sop_stubs_remaining == 0 and avg_sop_per_role > 0). A dept with status=done but sopLibraryFilled=false is INCOMPLETE — the SOP placeholders were never authored. Added v10.15.8. ENFORCED: see sopLibraryStatus + the verify/resume gate.

wiringStatus.description: ENFORCED wiring gate (added v12.8.3). Set by verify-wiring.sh ONLY (never hand-set). 'done' means this dept passed all four wiring assertions: (1) materialized - every role has a real how-to.md (>= 3KB, no [PENDING] marker); (2) registered - dept-<slug> agent is in openclaw.json agents.list with a valid workspace path; (3) reachable - a Director/Head/Lead/Architect entry-point role exists; (4) connected - all required keys in connection-manifest.json resolve in openclaw.json. 'failed' means one or more assertions failed; see wiringFailReasons. A dept with status=done but wiringStatus!=done is a STUB DEPT that must not reach closeout. The resume cron fires [WIRING-RESUME] until this is 'done' for every dept. buildCompletedAt MUST NOT be written while any dept has wiringStatus != done.

wiringCheckedAt.description: ISO timestamp of the last verify-wiring.sh run for this dept. Updated on every gate pass or fail.

wiringFailReasons.description: Space-separated list of named failure categories from the last failed wiring gate run (e.g. 'materialized:stub-1200B registered:agent-not-in-config'). Cleared when wiringStatus transitions to done.

agentsMaterializedCount.description: Count of dept entries actually present in openclaw.json agents.list[] (excludes the default 'main'). Written by Skill 37 Step 1 after running materialize-dept-agents.sh. Cross-check against the number of departments where status==done — a mismatch means the closeout MUST fail rather than send a celebration the runtime can't back up. Added v10.14.19.

openItems.description: Free-form notes about post-build cleanup, e.g. 'Vertical Pack confirmation pending', 'HITMCN merger decision pending'

resumeAttempts.description: Count of autonomous resume invocations. Capped at maxResumeAttempts to prevent infinite loops.

maxResumeAttempts.description: Hard cap. After this, resume-workforce-build.sh stops self-pinging and notifies Trevor instead of the owner.

roleLibraryStatus.description: ENFORCED build gate (added v10.15.8). Tracks whether the ROLE LIBRARY has been pulled into every role folder (how-to.md filled from templates/role-library/ via post-build-role-workspaces.py). Set to 'pending' the moment the build starts; 'pulling' while post-build augmentation runs; 'done' only when qc-completeness.sh reports library_pct == 100 across ALL on-disk depts (every per-dept roleLibraryFilled == true); 'failed' if the pull errored or coverage < 100. A workforce is NOT complete (buildCompletedAt MUST NOT be set, closeout MUST NOT fire) until roleLibraryStatus == done. The resume cron fires a [LIBRARY-RESUME] self-ping while this is pending/pulling/failed. Last-night incident (multiple clients): workforces were scaffolded but the role library was never connected — this field + gate prevent that.

sopLibraryStatus.description: ENFORCED build gate (added v10.15.8). Tracks whether the SOP LIBRARY has been authored/pulled for every role (SOP files written, no 'to be personalized' stubs remaining), via populate-sops-from-manifest.py. Set to 'pending' at build start; 'authoring' while SOP population runs; 'done' only when qc-completeness.sh reports sop_stubs_remaining == 0 and avg_sop_per_role > 0 across ALL on-disk depts (every per-dept sopLibraryFilled == true); 'failed' if population errored or stubs remain. A workforce is NOT complete until sopLibraryStatus == done. The resume cron fires a [LIBRARY-RESUME] self-ping while this is pending/authoring/failed. Same last-night incident class as roleLibraryStatus.

libraryFailureReason.description: Captures the specific reason role/SOP library population failed or is incomplete (e.g. 'role-library: marketing 4/12 how-to.md filled', 'sop: 18 stubs remaining', 'populate-sops-from-manifest.py rc=3 owner-input-required'). Added v10.15.8.

librariesNearCapNotified.description: Throttle flag (added v10.15.9). Set true the FIRST time resume-workforce-build.sh emits an OPERATOR-FACING 'library gate not closing' status line — i.e. when the libraries are still dirty AND resumeAttempts has reached within 2 of maxResumeAttempts. Surfacing this near-cap (instead of only at the hard cap) makes a persistently-failing role-library/SOP pull visible before the cron stops retrying. Once true, the status is not re-emitted on every remaining cycle.

commsAutomationStatus.description: ENFORCED cross-skill chain to Skill 38 (added v10.15.9). When the built workforce includes a Communications, Sales, or Customer-Support department, the closeout MUST hand off to Skill 38 (conversational AI system) to scaffold the matching comms automations — the same enforcement shape as the role/SOP library gate (state field + verify/resume self-ping), NOT prose. Set by the Skill 23 master orchestrator at Moment 3.8 once all departments are done: 'not-applicable' if NONE of Communications/Sales/Customer-Support was built (no comms department => no handoff needed); otherwise 'pending'. Skill 38 transitions pending → scaffolding → done. 'done' ONLY when the client's conversation-workflows/ has at least the appointment-booking starter playbook + its Build-with-AI prompt registered (THE TRINITY) — verifiable via Skill 38 scripts/qc-trinity-registry.sh. A workforce with a comms/sales/support department but commsAutomationStatus NOT IN {done, not-applicable} is NOT fully delivered — resume-workforce-build.sh treats it as dirty and dispatches a [COMMS-AUTOMATION-RESUME] self-ping. Last-night incident class (siblings with zero cross-references): a Sales/Support workforce shipped with no conversational automations wired.

commsAutomationDepartments.description: The department slugs that triggered the Skill 38 handoff (e.g. ['communications','sales','customer-support']). Written by Skill 23 alongside commsAutomationStatus so the resume self-ping can name exactly which department(s) need comms automations. Added v10.15.9.

commsAutomationVerifiedAt.description: When the Skill 38 trinity check (qc-trinity-registry.sh) last confirmed the matching comms automations are registered. Written when commsAutomationStatus is set to 'done'. Added v10.15.9.

closeoutStatus.description: Skill 37 ZHC Closeout status. Set by Skill 23 master orchestrator to 'pending' the moment buildCompletedAt is written. Skill 37 transitions pending → generating → sent → done. failed = retries exhausted; resume cron will retry. partial = soft-only failures (video/notion); blocked-* = build prerequisites not met. Added in v10.14.17. NOTE (v10.15.8): the master orchestrator MUST NOT write buildCompletedAt or set closeoutStatus=pending until roleLibraryStatus == done AND sopLibraryStatus == done. The library gate runs BEFORE the closeout gate. PRD-2.8: seven per-deliverable fields in closeoutDeliverables track individual leg completion; closeoutStatus is the aggregate. blocked-interview-incomplete = interviewQc.status != pass AND the interview is NOT complete (interviewComplete != true). blocked-qc-pending = interviewQc.status != pass but interviewComplete == true (a QC verdict gap on a FINISHED interview, not an incomplete interview) - added to stop the false-negative that reported completed interviews as 'interview incomplete'.

closeoutDeliverables.description: PRD-2.8: per-deliverable boolean/status fields for the 7 closeout legs. Each field is 'done' when that leg has been delivered and verified, 'waived' when the operator explicitly skips it, or 'failed' when exhausted. The resume cron fires until all are done|waived. This is the per-leg detail; closeoutStatus is the aggregate. Added v11.10.0.

notionTreeUrl.description: Notion 9-section page tree root URL once delivered (matches .notionRootPageUrl when set)

infographic1Url.description: Workforce-structure org chart URL (HTML/Playwright render). Set once the artifact passes the 8.5 gate and has been delivered.

infographic2Url.description: How-Work-Flows diagram URL (KIE.AI / Nano Banana 2). Set once artifact passes 8.5 gate and delivered.

celebrationVideoUrl.description: Celebration video URL (local path delivered via Telegram inline, or durable GHL URL). Set after byte-upload confirmed.

telegramSequenceSent.description: True once all 6 paced Telegram messages have been delivered AND confirmed against the gateway sent-registry (verify-telegram-delivery.sh passes). This is the final leg the owner sees.

ccUrlDelivered.description: True once the Command Center URL (commandCenterUrl) has been built and posted to the owner in Message 6 of the Telegram sequence.

n8nWired.description: True or 'skipped' once the n8n wire-up step completes (POST to client n8n webhook with company slug, agent name, and CC URL). 'skipped' when N8N_WEBHOOK_URL is absent and ZHC_SKIP_N8N=1.

closeoutBlockReason.description: Set when closeoutStatus is blocked-floor-incomplete or blocked-libraries-incomplete. Human-readable reason string.

closeoutPartialReason.description: Set when closeoutStatus is partial (phantom-closeout guard). Human-readable reason string.

closeoutResumeRegisteredAt.description: PRD-2.8: ISO timestamp when the dedicated closeout-resume cron was last registered via `openclaw cron add`. Written by install-closeout-resume-cron.sh and by run-closeout.sh on first start. Used to detect whether the dedicated cron is alive.

closeoutResumeUuid.description: PRD-2.8: OpenClaw cron UUID for the dedicated closeout-resume cron (resume-closeout-cron.sh). Written at registration. Used for self-removal when closeout reaches done|partial.

interviewNudgeRegisteredAt.description: v12.3.10: ISO timestamp when the interview-nudge cron was last registered via `openclaw cron add`. Written by install.sh install_interview_nudge_cron(). Used to detect whether the nudge cron is alive.

interviewNudgeUuid.description: v12.3.10: OpenClaw cron UUID for the interview-nudge cron (interview-nudge-cron.sh). Written at registration. Used for self-removal when interviewComplete=true reaches closeoutStatus=done (run-closeout.sh) and on the next cron fire (interview-nudge-cron.sh self_remove_cron).

closeoutStartedAt.description: When Skill 37 first transitioned pending → generating

closeoutFailureReason.description: Captures the specific step that exhausted retries (e.g. 'command-center: 3 attempts exhausted', 'infographic-1: ...', 'notion: ...')

closeoutPartialArtifacts.description: Set when closeoutStatus=partial. Names the soft-failed legs (e.g. ['celebration-video', 'notion']). Hard-failed legs are in closeoutFailureReason.

closeoutPendingSlots.description: BUG-A FIX (ghost-closeout): names the closeout legs still NOT verified-complete when closeoutStatus is failed/partial (e.g. ['infographic-1','telegram']). Cleared to [] only on a verified closeoutStatus=done. Written by run-closeout.sh finalize and resume-closeout-cron.sh; lets operators and the resume cron see exactly which critical legs are outstanding.

closeoutCriticalFailed.description: BUG-A FIX (ghost-closeout): STICKY marker listing the critical legs (infographic-1 / infographic-2 / telegram) that failed in the last run-closeout.sh finalize. Set when closeoutStatus=failed; removed only when run-closeout.sh writes a VERIFIED done. resume-closeout-cron.sh reads this (and closeoutStatus=failed) and REFUSES to self-stamp closeoutStatus=done over it — the cron may only re-launch run-closeout.sh, whose own phantom-guard + telegram-delivery-confirmation gate is the only path to done.

commandCenterStatus.description: Skill 32 Command Center setup status (tracked by Skill 37 Step 1). 'done-degraded' (Issue #6 fail-closed) means the dashboard is locally up but one or more required board-provisioning sub-phases (6b workspaces / 6c departments / 6d md-content / 6e dashboard-content) did not reach true, or the ZHE acceptance gate failed under the ZHE_ENFORCE=0 escape hatch; the resume cron revisits it because it is not 'done'.

commandCenterDegradedPhases.description: Issue #6: comma-joined list of the required Command Center sub-phases that did not reach true on the last run-full-install pass (empty/null when commandCenterStatus=done). Operator-facing surface for the resume cron.

commandCenterUrl.description: Resolved URL of the Command Center (e.g. http://localhost:4000)

n8nStatus.description: n8n integration wire-up status (optional — may be 'skipped' for clients without n8n)

n8nUrl.description: Client's n8n instance URL if wired up

infographic1Url.description: KIE.AI-returned URL for the Workforce Structure infographic

infographic2Url.description: KIE.AI-returned URL for the How Work Flows infographic

celebrationVideoUrl.description: KIE.AI-returned URL for the celebration video (Gemini Omni Video, model id gemini-omni-video; Veo 3.1 fallback). This is the EPHEMERAL vendor CDN URL (aiquickdraw) -- not durable. The bytes are downloaded to celebrationVideoLocalPath and, where GHL is present, re-hosted to a durable public URL in ghlVideoPublicUrl.

celebrationVideoLocalPath.description: Local on-disk path to the downloaded celebration MP4 (workspace/.zhc-celebration-video.mp4). Sent inline to Telegram via multipart and uploaded to the GHL media library.

celebrationVideoModel.description: The KIE model that actually produced the celebration video (gemini-omni-video | veo3 | veo3_fast).

infographic1LocalPath.description: Local on-disk path to the rendered Workforce Structure org-chart PNG (workspace/.zhc-inf1-output.png). Sent inline to Telegram and uploaded to GHL.

infographic2LocalPath.description: Local on-disk path to the How-Work-Flows infographic PNG, when downloaded for GHL re-hosting.

ghlLocationId.description: Convert and Flow / GHL LOCATION (sub-account) id for this client, if provisioned. Closeout media uploads target this location. Empty when the client has no GHL location (do NOT fabricate).

ghlMediaUploaded.description: true when closeout media was uploaded to the client's GHL media library; otherwise a skip/failure reason string (skipped-no-ghl | skipped-no-pit | skipped-pit-verify-failed | skipped-no-files | failed-no-files-accepted).

ghlMediaFileIds.description: GHL fileIds of every closeout media file accepted by /medias/upload-file.

ghlMediaUrls.description: PUBLIC (storage.googleapis.com/msgsndr/...) URLs of every uploaded closeout media file -- openable without a GHL login.

ghlMediaLibraryUrl.description: In-app Convert and Flow media-storage link (behind GHL login) where the closeout media lives. Convenience 'browse them all' link -- NOT the openable artifact link.

ghlVideoPublicUrl.description: Durable, openable PUBLIC URL of the celebration video re-hosted in the GHL media library. Posted to Telegram as the video's 'open it directly' link.

ghlInfographic1PublicUrl.description: Durable, openable PUBLIC URL of the Workforce Structure infographic re-hosted in the GHL media library.

ghlInfographic2PublicUrl.description: Durable, openable PUBLIC URL of the How-Work-Flows infographic re-hosted in the GHL media library.

ghlMediaUploadedAt.description: Timestamp the GHL media upload completed.

notionRootPageUrl.description: URL of the root Notion closeout page in the client's workspace

messageId.description: Real gateway Telegram messageId returned by `openclaw message send --json`. Non-empty == confirmed-accepted by the gateway. A slot is only counted delivered when this is present and non-empty.

status.description: Set to 'send-failed' when the gateway returned no messageId; such a slot is NOT counted delivered and is retried.

oneOf.description: Legacy (pre-v10.15.19/v10.16.18) bare-integer form. Still accepted for backward compatibility.

messagesDelivered.description: Per-message Telegram delivery tracker for Step 6 of Skill 37. As of v10.15.19/v10.16.18 each entry is an OBJECT { n, messageId, chatId, ts } carrying the REAL gateway messageId so the closeout can cross-check delivery against the sent-registry (verify-telegram-delivery.sh) before claiming done. A slot with no messageId is recorded { n, status:'send-failed' } and is not counted delivered. Bare integers from older runs are still accepted.

telegramDeliveryVerification.description: Result of verify-telegram-delivery.sh cross-checking captured messageIds against the gateway sent-registry. closeoutStatus=done is gated on status=='pass'.

industryPack.description: PRD-2.15: provenance of the industry vertical pack the interview ran. Written by 23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/record-industry-pack.sh (which calls shared-utils/industry-detector.py). build-workforce.py REQUIRES industryPack.slug before it will build — an interview with no recorded pack is not industry-custom and must not proceed. If slug='unknown', build-workforce.py warns loudly but does not hard-block (an unclassifiable business is still buildable). Added v11.11.0.

source.description: auto-detected when confidence>=0.7 and owner did not change it; owner-confirmed/corrected when the C-4 / Phase 5 confirmation ran

interviewQc.description: PRD-2.15: result of 23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/qc-interview-completion.py. closeoutStatus MUST NOT advance past 'pending' until interviewQc.status=='pass'. Folds the interview experience into the 2.8 closeout gate so 'interview felt great AND artifacts landed' is one verified state. Set to {status:'pending'} by update-interview-state.sh --complete. Added v11.11.0. Cross-skill seam (WIRED): run-closeout.sh (Skill 37) gates on interviewQc.status != 'pass' before proceeding (the interviewQc HARD GATE in run-closeout.sh).

legacyExemption.description: v12.4.0: result of the legacy/pre-standard count-floor exemption in qc-interview-completion.py. 'claimed' = the interview was flagged legacy (via --legacy-interview, state.legacyInterview.preStandard, or a transcript marker). 'granted' = the exemption was actually applied (claimed AND passed the anti-fabrication substance floor). The exemption lifts ONLY the 25-35 count floor; jargon/mandatory-field/no-fabrication checks always still apply.

sources.description: Evidence the interview was flagged legacy, e.g. ['state:legacyInterview.preStandard'], ['transcript:legacy-marker'], ['cli:--legacy-interview'].

legacyInterview.description: v12.4.0: marks a VERIFIED pre-standard / legacy interview that predates the 25-35 question standard. When preStandard==true (and the interview passes the anti-fabrication substance floor), qc-interview-completion.py exempts it from the check #1 count floor WITHOUT re-interviewing the client. This is the 'don't re-interview real clients' path: it applies ONLY to genuine, owner-authored interviews, never to empty/fabricated ones (an empty transcript carrying this flag still HARD-FAILs with 'legacy-flag-without-substance'). The remaining fields are an optional audit trail.

preStandard.description: true = this is a verified interview that predates the 25-35 question standard; eligible for the count-floor exemption.

ownerConfirmed.description: true = the owner has confirmed this legacy interview is genuine and complete.

standardVersion.description: The pre-standard the interview was conducted under, for the audit trail (e.g. 'pre-25Q').

interviewProgress.description: Per-question state updated by update-interview-state.sh. Source of truth for the resume cron and dashboard interview-progress UI. Added in v10.15.1 (VPS) / v10.14.1 (Mac). v12.3.4: added contextIngest sub-object.

contextIngest.description: v12.3.4: written by context-ingest.py (Phase 0.5). Records that the context pre-pass ran and how many themes it classified. Non-blocking: absent = pre-pass not yet run. Used by QC and dashboard to show context-ingestion status.

sources.description: List of source file labels that were present during ingestion.

themesKnown.description: Number of interview themes classified as KNOWN (confirmed, not re-asked cold).

themesPartial.description: Number of interview themes classified as PARTIAL (deepened).

themesUnknown.description: Number of interview themes classified as UNKNOWN (asked fresh).

mapPath.description: Absolute path to the interview-context-map.json written by context-ingest.py.

lastQuestionNumber.description: DEPRECATED v10.15.1 / v10.14.1. Use interviewProgress.lastQuestionNumber. Kept tolerated for legacy state files.

interviewStalled.description: PRD-2.15 (v12.3.12): set by nudge-incomplete-interviews.py after 168h nudge dead-end. Consumed by closeout-readiness-watchdog as a strong STUCK_MID_INTERVIEW signal.

interviewStalledAt.description: ISO timestamp when interviewStalled was first set.

closeoutBlockers.description: PRD-2.15 (v12.3.12): durable operator surface. Each entry records a stuck condition that was escalated. Appended by closeout-readiness-watchdog.sh and run-closeout.sh on hard holds. Cleared when the condition resolves.

stuckEscalations.description: PRD-2.15 (v12.3.12): per-class throttle ledger written by closeout-readiness-watchdog. Prevents duplicate escalations. Keys are STUCK_CLASS names; values have notifiedAt (ISO) + class.

closeoutLegStatus.description: PRD-2.15 (v12.3.12): per-leg human-readable status map (e.g. {org_chart:'failed:playwright-missing', notion:'skipped:no-page-id'}). Gives operator a single field to read why each closeout leg is red.

infographic1FailureReason.description: PRD-2.15 (v12.3.12): structured failure reason for the org-chart infographic leg. Written by generate-infographics.sh on Playwright-missing or render failure. Example: 'playwright-missing: chromium not installed on this host'.

notionFailureReason.description: PRD-2.15 (v12.3.12): structured failure reason for the Notion closeout leg. Written by create-notion-closeout.sh on root-page 401/scope failure.

notionCloseoutPageId.description: PRD-2.15 (v12.3.12): Notion page ID of the root closeout page, set by create-notion-closeout.sh on first successful run (alongside notionRootPageUrl). The --refresh-workforce-only path reads this to avoid its 'skip forever' failure mode.

canonicalReconciliation.description: PRD R2.x (v12.5.x reconciliation engine): the auditable record of how the owner's department set was reconciled with the canonical floor (23 mandatory + 6 universal-primary = 29, computed live). TIERS (never conflate the floor with the total): 29 = guaranteed FLOOR (23 mandatory + 6 universal-primary); 34 = built departments (role-library/_index.json); 45 = full catalog union in department-naming-map.json; no client gets all 34/45 — each gets the 29 floor plus keyword-matched industry verticals minus declines; always derive live, never restate a bare integer. Naming-map v2.6.1 reclassified listings to real-estate-only, removing it from the universal_primary layer and dropping the universal-primary count from 7 to 6 and the floor from 29 to 28 (mandatory count of 22 unchanged); v2.6.2 (2026-07-16, operator ruling) added 'funnels' as the 23rd mandatory dept, raising the floor back to 29. Written by build-workforce.py reconcile_canonical_floor / apply_semantic_merges / materialize_custom_roles / capture_custom_sops and read by department-floor.py.

decisions.description: Per-dept opt-in/opt-out: id -> 'yes'|'no'|'later'. Covers mandatory canonical depts AND the 6 universal-primary vertical depts (naming-map v2.6.1: listings reclassified to real-estate-only) AND custom depts (R2.6 symmetric opt-out). A 'no' is the ONLY sanctioned way below the floor; honored by both build-workforce.py and department-floor.py.

mergeDecisions.description: R2.3 Capability 2 semantic merge: custom_id -> 'merge'|'keep'. 'merge' folds the custom dept INTO the overlapping canonical dept (one department, no duplicate); 'keep' ships both standalone. An un-decided overlap stays standalone and is recorded under semanticMerges.pending.

customKeeps.description: R2.2 Capability 1: non-canonical departments the owner explicitly asked for that have no floor equivalent (preserved as standalone).

customRoles.description: R2.4 Capability 3 INPUT: dept_id -> list of extra roles the owner asked for (string title or {title, summary, permanent}). Materialized at build by materialize_custom_roles().

customRolesBuilt.description: R2.4 Capability 3 AUDIT: the custom roles actually materialized at build ({dept, title, slug, permanent}).

customSops.description: R2.5 Capability 4 INPUT: dept_id -> list of owner-specific procedures (string or {title, procedure}). Captured at build by capture_custom_sops() respecting sop_boundary_gate.py.

customSopsCaptured.description: R2.5 Capability 4 AUDIT: procedures captured ({dept, title, isCanonical, file}). isCanonical=true => supplemental overlay over copied library SOPs (LLM authoring refused); false => LLM-authoring ground truth for a custom dept.

semanticMerges.description: R2.3 AUDIT: {proposals, merged, kept, pending, evaluatedAt, source} from apply_semantic_merges().

mergedInto.description: R2.3 build-time layering carrier: survivor canonical dept id -> list of absorbed custom dept records ({id, name, description, base_suggested_roles}) so the survivor materializes the absorbed function as a role inside itself.

verticalPacks.description: Auditable vertical-pack application record written by build-workforce.py apply_vertical_packs(): {detectedPacks, addedDepartments, skippedDuplicates, declinedVerticals, researchManifest, appliedAt, source}. declinedVerticals (R2.6) lists universal-primary verticals the owner opted out of, skipped at build.
